Notes / Memories: First live music since shows were cancelled in March 2020. Widely spaced table seating in an outdoor tent only.
Lawn seats — no videos.
Still photos only — no video footage.
Primarily there for Larkin Poe — shot their full set at Outlaw Music Festival, including their set closer Come on in my Kitchen (Robert Johnson cover).
Seated last row — too far; no usable video footage. Great schtick, but they miss Dusty a ton
City Winery DC. Shot several tracks including Shangri-La, Dryden Mine, Letters, and Salvation.
Tab's birthday show. He came out and said: "It's my birthday! That means I can play as loud as I want!" Also caught the Dirty Dozen Brass Band opening set.
Kate played several unreleased tracks from the then-forthcoming Fish Bowl album, available at the time only for pre-order on Bandcamp.
Band included Buthel Burns (bass), Cedric Goodman (drums), Brad Walker (saxophone), Jon Ramm-Gramenz (trumpet).
30th Anniversary Flood Show — previously scheduled for 4/8/20, 12/10/20, 4/10/21, 3/4/22, and 6/10/22 before finally happening. Shot from the rail at Lincoln Theatre.
Encore featured brass members from Spring Heeled Jack and Eastern Standard Time. An audience member who got pushed off stage was frog-marched out of the venue.

George Clinton lent some gravitas mid-jam to Constance Hauman and Blu Eye Extinction during their opening set on NYE. Greg Thomas jammed through the countdown on sax and Parliament gave us an Auld Lang Syne while balloons dropped from the ceiling.
Full show on Kennedy Center YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/4DiQkmO8kGc
Daniel Donato opened and joined Greensky for an Allman Brothers cover (Ain't Wastin' Time No More) and Give a Shhh. Shot Daniel's full opening set.
Pat Murphy did a Snoop Dogg impression during Johnny Tarr. Not even kidding.
House of Hamill performed a CSN Helplessly Hoping in the middle of the Enter the Haggis set.

Shot Megan Lovell solo-only clip of Might as Well be Me. Rebecca Lovell did the Mad as a Hatter intro. Shot from the 9:30 Club. This version of Preachin' Blues sustains the channel earnings basically by itself
Triple bill at Songbyrd — opener Flo Petite covered Nirvana (In Bloom).
VIP sound check included Calypso, Caramel, and Marlene on the Wall before the main set.
Set my phone down on the table — Eric and band had the floor thumping all night. Auto-focus was fairly terrible on a couple of clips.
Shot primarily Wood Brothers' full opening set from the 2nd level balcony. Wood Brothers brought Shovels & Rope back out for an encore CCR cover (Down on the Corner).
Front row at the Birchmere. Lindsay Lou covered Angel of Montgomery and Amazing Grace with the trio. Also caught her solo opening set.
Larkin Poe opened and joined Indigo Girls for Shame on Me during the set and Closer to Fine during the encore. Pavilion stage left row G.
Shot primarily Samantha Fish & Jesse Dayton and Robert Randolph sets supporting Buddy Guy. The two acts joined together for a Going Down curtain call.

Stood next to the photographer — that had its drawbacks. Shot almost the full Kate Davis set with band, plus Mystery Friends and THYGUI. Senator Schumer was in the audience, he had a staffer who played in one of the other two bands
Technical difficulties — only slow-motion videos; no usable footage.

From the rail at The 8x10 Baltimore. Got to fix Kingfish's cord chaos on his pedalboard while he was playing — a proud moment for this former "black shirt." A kid in the crowd came out behind me to take a shot at part of a solo — pretty sure it was his nephew (brother who "moved off to Baltimore" definitely confirmed).
Shot Kingfish Ingram's full support set from the pavilion balcony. Shot Steve Miller walkout and a couple of songs.
Shot from the side of stage — big head dude between me and the front on multiple clips.
Tori told a story about being abruptly fired from a piano bar job in DC on July 4th, 1983 — she showed up in costume and someone else was already playing. Pavilion center-left aisle, row U.
Penultimate row of the upper balcony at Baltimore Lyric. Deep cuts from the early set. Also caught one Nick Lowe & Los Straitjackets track.
From the rail in front of Mustang 🤘 Two-set show at The 8x10.
Front row corner at the Birchmere. Tab opened with a little "bonus" lagniappe improv while the gumbo was cooking. Medicine turned into an epic jam. He riffed extensively making fun of Ben Franklin.
Shot primarily King Solomon Hicks' opening set — a full 7-track playlist. Jimmie Vaughan: dude is 72 and still doing a helluva show.

Steve introduced My Old Friend the Blues as "Gary (Oelze, founder of the Birchmere)'s favorite song of mine" — Gary had passed away in January. David Simon (The Wire, Tremé) was at the table to my right.
2nd row, shooting low. Band: Richard Cousins (bass), Dover Weinberg (keyboards), Les Falconer (drums).
Album drop day for Dirty Shine — shot from the rail. Jaime Wyatt opened with a Led Zeppelin cover (Ramble On). Hat autograph: ZZ Ward.(first to sign)
Set opener at Rosslyn Jazz Fest before thunderstorms washed out the rest of the bands. Also attended Kingsley Flood at Pearl Street Warehouse the same night.

Two shows same day: Kingsley Flood at Pearl Street Warehouse (Glenn Yoder supporting, Lucinda Williams cover in the set), then DuPont Brass at Rosslyn Jazz Fest — only one song before thunderstorms washed out the rest of the lineup.
Final show of the Summer Odyssey tour. "They let it all hang out." Also caught Sir Chloe and Phoenix. Shot from Row B, stage left, behind the pit.
Back porch style — opened with Blues Attack. Peter Case supporting. Shot from the front row at Hamilton Live.
Upper deck at Capital One Arena. RZA told the white folks in the audience what to say in place of a particular word in the lyrics during the Wu-Tang set.
Side box, trying to be subtle — lots of shiftiness in the footage. Band: Jason Sypher (bass), Francesco Turrisi, Dirk Powell, Niwel Tsumbu (guitar). Adia Victoria also on the bill.
Shot from stage left balcony above the band. Rebirth Brass Band opened, then joined the Wooten Brothers for the final jam.
Front table at Strathmore AMP. "Next time I think I need to sit in front of Laura Chavez!!!"
The F.O.R.C.E. Live Tour. LL Cool J gave a nice shout out to Chuck Brown and DC go-go. Caught Queen Latifah with Monie Love, The Roots + DJ Jazzy Jeff, and Wu-Tang.
Balcony at Lincoln Theater. They covered Seal's Crazy.
Front table at the Birchmere. They played I've Got Your Back, an unreleased track from their forthcoming album.
Stage right balcony at 9:30 Club. Opened Hard Wired with a Beatles cover (Blue Jay Way).
Guyville Tour, upper balcony at The Anthem. Also shot Blondshell opening set — favorite lyric from their set: "it was nice what you said, but it doesn't carry weight."

VIP pre-show meet & greet at The Barns — unamplified, no mics. They did a Dean Martin cover (Marshmallow World) and answered fan Q&A. Shot from in front of Kanene for the main set. Hat autograph: Kanene Pipkin (of The Lone Bellow).

Death Wish Blues tour. Set opened with MC5 cover (Kick Out the Jams). Also shot Townes Van Zandt cover (I'll Be Here in the Morning) and cigar box guitar duet. Jon Spencer and Eric Johanson also on the bill. Hat autograph: Samantha Fish.
Screamin' Jay Hawkins cover (I Put a Spell on You). Also caught the Dirty Dozen Brass Band on the bill.
All-star night: featured guests included Tal Wilkenfeld, Jimmy Hall, Ally Venable, and Ghalia Volt. 3rd row stage left, lots of heads and arms in the way.
Hat autograph: Allison Russell.
First visit to The Atlantis. "Deep Dive Live" triple bill (Brassie, Glom, Sadurn). Very singalong neighbor for Sadurn.
Shot from the rail and balcony. They covered Journey, Naked Eyes, Michael Jackson, The Cars, and B-52s among many others.
Full show on Kennedy Center YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/79R31AnZcrY
Mid-floor left center aisle at CFG Bank Arena. She opened with a Buffalo Springfield cover (For What It's Worth). Jessie Baylin supported.
Artur Menezes opened and supported throughout. They brought Crown the poodle out from under Ladonna's feet during I Want My Crown. Hat autograph: Poliana 'Pops' Magalhães (w/Artur Menezes Band).
Stage left balcony at 9:30 Club. She did a short acoustic segment mid-set. Becca Mancari opened.
Full show on Kennedy Center YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/NeWAJJGdUdw
Front table at Collective Encore. Opened with a lap steel Saints Go Marching In. Strong set including Nobody Loves Me but my Mother.
Walked out to Also Sprach Zarathustra (Richard Strauss). Closed with an MC Hammer cover (Turn This Mutha Out). 6th row center. Soul Rebels opened.
Trevor Lewington was absent due to a family medical issue — the set list was not as planned. Shot from a front table at Hamilton Live.
Hat autograph: Charly Lowry (w/Rhiannon Giddens).
Shot on the Spring Equinox from 5th row center. Trombone Shorty made a special unbilled appearance. The drum finale had both bands' tour drummers and Trombone Shorty all on kit simultaneously.
Front table at Collective Encore. Grabbed mostly deep cuts, but they did all the hits plus a couple of Sting solo tracks. Cha!
Hat autograph: Al Olender (w/Shovels & Rope).
Extended drum solo from Go Go Ray. Also shot Mallow Hill's opening set — first use of the external mic. Hat autograph: Danielle Nicole.
Shot from the rail. The loud woman next to me had an intense and slightly drunken crush on JJ. Judith Hill opened — she was extraordinary. Hat autograph: Judith Hill.
Hat autograph: Vanessa Collier.
Hat autograph: Joanne Shaw Taylor.
Triple bill with The North Country and Wild Child. Stage right balcony at 9:30 Club.
Still photos only — no video footage.
Hat autograph: Jackie Venson ([#12]).
Front table at the Birchmere. He gave a birthday shout out to Wes the guitar tech. Rebecca Porter opened and played throughout the set. Hat autograph: Rebecca Porter (w/John Hiatt).
Lawn at Merriweather. It rained. Obstructed view — caught some Cat Power, Modest Mouse, and Pixies despite conditions.
Shot primarily Danielle Ponder's opening set — she covered Radiohead (Creep). Also caught the Trombone Shorty second line and a Chuck Berry cover (Wind Me Up, Chuck).
Microphone technical difficulties — no usable video footage.
Small crowd included two dudes with a talent for standing directly between the camera and the performers. Spencer Tate on mandolin and violin throughout.
Hat autograph: Mikaela Davis.
Front table in front of Cesar Rosas. He introduced I Got Loaded with "we got a deal on quinceañeras right now." They played Bertha (Grateful Dead) by audience request.
Tab dedicated Forever My Love to John Brinegar (1964-2024), despite audience members shouting out Gary Oelze.
First of two nights at Warner Theatre. Shot from the balcony before the ushers got militant on Chrissie Hynde's behalf.

S.E.R.P.E.N.T. Festival at Koka Booth Amphitheatre — shot from row 4 in the rain. Robert Randolph opened with Eric Gales as an unbilled guest on Foxy Lady (Hendrix cover). ZZ Ward, Larkin Poe, and Slash followed. Rebecca Lovell gave me a personal shout out from the stage for wearing my Bluephoria T-Shirt before they played the song "just for you" - I had bought it right away when the song was released and they had spotted me in the VIP entrance line when their tour bus pulled in
Front table at Hamilton Live including some solo acoustic. Four videos were later removed due to a copyright strike claim from Sue Foley herself. Hat autograph: Sue Foley.
His tribute to John Mayall was Say Goodbye to the Blues. Band: Micheal Leasure (drums), John Avila (bass), Richard T Bear (keyboards), Brett Smith Daniels (guitar).
Beanstalk Library opened with a Stevie Nicks cover (Stop Draggin' My Heart Around). Kingsley played a Clash cover (Career Opportunities). Shot from the balcony.
She premiered a then-unreleased track (Take Me Back). Tongue Tied was played by audience request. Hat autograph: Laura Chavez (w/Vanessa Collier).
Front row at The Vault. Carly Harvey opened and covered Fiona Apple (Misery). Selwyn's Saints Go Marching In extended jam was extraordinary. Hat autograph: Carly Harvey (w/Selwyn Birchwood).
He opened with a Jimmy Cliff quote (Many Rivers to Cross) and closed with an Allman Brothers cover (Ramblin' Man) as the encore.
Hat autograph: Suzanne Vega.
Tyson's Corner Mall jingle and Spotsylvania Mall jingle between songs. They covered Paul Simon (Slip Slidin' Away) and riffed on Ventura Highway during tuning.
Front row at Publick Playhouse. Several track titles I couldn't identify — posted on YouTube asking for help. Hat autograph: Valerie June.

Why Don't We Duet? — benefit concert for the Potter's House Pay It Forward program. 18 local musicians paired by Tarot card (Dame Darcy's Mermaid Deck) into 9 duos, each writing and debuting a new song. $15 at the door, 7:30–11:30 PM. Talia was paired with Nina Goodman (keyboardist/singer, DC Music Review writer) — they both drew the Knight of Pentacles. They were the first duo announced on social media but not the first to perform. Their song was about the joy of being a dog — there was both joy and actual barking in it. Afterwards Talia was so disappointed in her voice (due to cancer treatments) that she asked me not to post the bootleg video I took. Hat autograph: Talia Segal (RIP).
He opened with a Duke Ellington cover (Jump for Joy) and noted that Duke Ellington was a District native. David Moore supported. Carter Family, Duke Ellington, and traditional covers throughout.
Hat autograph: Ana Popović.

Front row extreme left, shooting into the light. She told the story of writing Cadillac Blue for her husband even though his eyes are hazel — then said she'd sing it for me since I raised my hand when she asked who had blue eyes. Hat autograph: Shemekia Copeland.

With Mali Obomsawin at Kennedy Center Millennium Stage. Covered Sister Rosetta Thorpe and Skip James. Full set on the Kennedy Center YouTube channel. Full show on Kennedy Center YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/9-vDzbKrb-Y Hat autograph: Mali Obomsawin (co-bill w/Jake Blount).
From front row of orchestra tier. Lindsay Lou supported. Hat autograph: Lindsay Lou (w/Wood Brothers [#24]).

Ruf Records 30th Anniversary Tour. Triple bill: Ghalia Volt (with Canned Heat + Bernard Allison), then Samantha Fish (with Mitch Ryder). Shot from stage left front row. Hat autograph: Ghalia Volt. Laura (w/Samantha Fish). Laura Chavez was there at the merch table but not playing. We chatted briefly
Hat autograph: CJ Johnson (of Oh He Dead).
State Theatre VA. Shot two tracks.
Three Sunday night sets at JV's Restaurant including sit-ins from Bobby Thompson and Linwood Taylor.
The Feicin' Freezin' Tour. Shot from the balcony. La Unica opened and also covered the Pogues (If I Should Fall From Grace With God) alongside Young Dubliners in a collaborative moment.

Tour kickoff. First set was Oliver Wood solo, second set was Langhorne Slim solo, then they played together to finish. Covered Leadbelly (Rock Island Line), Leonard Cohen (Tonight Will Be Fine), and Neil Young (Love is a Rose). There were jacket jokes.

Galactic featured vocalist Jelly Joseph throughout. Jah Works opened and covered Kyez. Galactic opened with a Professor Longhair cover (Go to the Mardi Gras). Also covered Eugene McDaniels (Compared to What).
Amadou Kouyate played untitled djembe as part of the show. Yasmin encored with a Doc Watson cover. Set opener was Cliffwalk. Hat autograph: Yasmin Williams.

Farewell Tour "Exit the Haggis" at The Barns at Wolf Trap. Acoustic set mid-show. Encore was One Last Drink / Gutter Anthem. Hat autographs: Rose Baldino (of Enter the Haggis); Caroline Browning (of Enter the Haggis).
Hub City Vinyl, Hagerstown MD. Kelli Baker Band opened and supported. Encore was a Muddy Waters cover.
Dirty Sun Tour at the Birchmere. Liam St. John opened and supporting. Shot from a front table.

They had played somewhere in PA at a festival that morning. Shot from the front row at Ram's Head. Hat autographs: Tierinii Jackson (of Southern Avenue); Tikyra Jackson (of Southern Avenue); Ava Jackson (of Southern Avenue).

2nd row stage right at Warner Theatre. Highlights: Eric Johnson and Dylan Triplett (One Rainy Wish), Kingfish and Ally Venable (Killing Floor with Mato Nanji), Zakk Wylde and Henri Brown (All Along the Watchtower), Kingfish and Henri Brown medley (Them Changes / Get Up / We Gotta Live Together).
40th Anniversary tour at the Birchmere. 2nd row table. They covered Lightnin' Hopkins (Shinin' Moon) and The Velvet Underground (Sweet Jane). Also did The Rake.

Howard Theatre DC. Tiera Kennedy opened and covered Dolly Parton (9 to 5). Michael's keyboard blocked the view for a lot of the early set. Jeep Cherokee Laredo was played by audience request — not on the original set list.
Full show on Kennedy Center YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/live/_SN7kNg5Q54 Hat autograph: Muireann Bradley.

All Returners Tour. VIP soundcheck included Our Native Daughters cover (Polly Ann's Hammer) and a rehearsal version of Requiem plus a new track (Cold April). Shot from the center rail for the main set. Kara Jackson opened.

Shot from the rail. Amythyst Kiah opened with Black Myself and I Will Not Go Down. Also shot a second-angle clip of She's a Self-Made Man. Hat autographs: Rebecca Lovell (of Larkin Poe); Megan Lovell (of Larkin Poe); Amythyst Kiah (w/Larkin Poe [#36]).

With Lightnin' Luke and Clyde McGee of Bridge City Sinners sitting in throughout. Opened with a Robert Johnson cover (Drunken Hearted Man). Also covered Carter/Cash/Watson/Douglas (What Would You Give in Exchange for Your Soul).
Ram's Head. Opened encore with an instrumental SRV tribute. Also covered James Taylor (Steamroller Blues) and Janet Jackson (Black Cat). Hat autograph: Ally Venable.

Artist: TajMo: The Taj Mahal & Keb' Mo' Band
Notes / Memories: Shot from 3rd row at Wolf Trap. Abraham Alexander opened. Covered Jimmy Cox (Nobody Knows You When You're Down and Out) and Jontavious Willis (Shake Me in Your Arms Tonight).
Hat autographs: Georgie Fuller (of The Heavy Heavy); Judy Blank (w/The Heavy Heavy).
Playlist pulled due to copyright strike by Sue Foley — no videos remain on channel.
Ram's Head. Closing medley was extraordinary: Voodoo Child / Kashmir / Back in Black / Für Elise all woven together.
Shot primarily Kingfish Ingram and Judith Hill's supporting sets. Buddy Guy closed the show. Shot from Wolf Trap Filene Center.
Wolf Trap. Fastball and Sugar Ray opened. Fastball drummer was the Sugar Ray drummer; BNL keyboardist played with Sugar Ray. The cross-band connections were fun.
Hat autograph: Jax Hollow.
Jammin' Java. The Pinkerton Raid opened and covered Counting Crows (A Long December). Amythyst covered Vera Hall (Trouble So Hard) and In The Pines.
Lawn seats — no videos. M and Bee sat in the pavilion by themselves, got to watch World's Smallest Violin right behind their seats
The Atlantis DC. Blessing Offor opened and covered Prince (Purple Rain into Brighter Days). Tash Neal also sat in. Robert Randolph's band included the pedal steel gospel intensity he's known for.
100 level stage left at Jiffy Lube Live. Gigi Perez opened. Shot four songs including Work Song and Too Sweet. M & Bee
The Anthem. The Budos Band opened — instrumental set. Alabama Shakes encored with Be Mine.
Hat autograph: Meghan Parnell (of Bywater Call).
Hamilton Live. Cry Cry Cry turned into an epic jam. She encored with The Pepper Club.
Hamilton Live. J & The Causeways opened and covered David Bowie (Young Americans).
Lincoln Theatre. Encored with a Paul Simon / Waltzing Matilda medley. He covered Nat King Cole (Mona Lisa) and did a Guitar Boogie / Stevie's Blues medley.
Hamilton Live. Cole Larravide opened. Ruthie covered Mississippi John Hurt. Also performed Woke Up This Morning (With My Mind Stayed on Freedom). Hat autograph: Ruthie Foster ([#42]).

Final show of The Big Money Tour 2025, VIP meet & greet, got a compliment on my purple shirt and hat. Halloween night at The Anthem. Guest bassist Blu De Tiger, Desz Washington on vocals, Taz Niederauer on guitar. Covered Beyoncé (American Requiem / Freedom), Blind Willie Johnson (Nobody's Fault But Mine), and Ray Charles (Lonely Avenue). After the show he did a second line outside on the street.
The Barns at Wolf Trap. Cody played an extended electric washboard jam. Traditional and Robert Johnson covers throughout.

Union Stage DC. VIP preshow unamplified at the rail. They debuted two new tracks (Common Folk, Count on Me). Nicole Atkins opened. APS signed by all three: Zach Williams, Kanene Pipkin, and Brian Elmquist.
Maryland Hall, solo show. Anthony D'Amato opened. Keb' opened with Am I Wrong. Shot from stage right box. Sent APS backstage for autograph after.
Hat autograph: Aila Wildman (of The Wildmans). Had an opportunity to stay for the after party but felt old
Bethesda Theatre — her final Christmas show. Hip Snacks opened. She debuted Poison in My Well with Kara Durante. Sadler Vaden covered Little Drummer Boy. She covered Joni Mitchell (River).
Hat autograph: Mohini Dey.

Arrived just before 6, 12th in line. Walked in with Bob Lubbehusen and Ed Warburton — they saved a seat. She played one song using an all-white right-handed bass with an embedded working neon tube that had been built for Dusty Hill of ZZ Top.
